message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Driver rotation: Luca is Driver for Task #11">
Driver rotation complete. We're now working on Task #11: Implementation Step 1 — Navigation + Dashboard skeleton.

**Luca Palmieri** is the new Driver (the only agent who may modify files).
**Marty Cagan** has been respawned as a Reviewer.

Luca will be implementing US-GTD-8 from docs/user-stories.md. Please review his work as it progresses and send feedback to luca-palmieri from your area of expertise. Key areas to watch:
- Kent: TDD discipline, test-first approach
- Scott: Domain types for routes and navigation state
- Carson: HTMX patterns, hx-boost, route structure
- Lea: CSS for header nav, flexbox wrap, design tokens
- Steve Schoger: Visual hierarchy, spacing, nav design
- Steve Krug: Usability of navigation, trunk test
- Heydon: Nav landmark, aria-current, keyboard nav, skip link
- Marty: Match against US-GTD-8 acceptance criteria
</teammate-message>

content.text: Understood. I've already completed my initial review of Luca's work in progress and sent detailed product feedback. Let me check if there have been any updates since my last review.

content.text: Excellent. Luca has addressed several of my earlier feedback items. Let me also check the new `gtd_placeholders.rs` and see what the placeholder routes look like.
Let me also check the placeholder template.
Good progress. Let me send updated product feedback to Luca addressing the new work.

input.content:
Luca, nice progress. I see you've addressed several items from my earlier feedback:

**Now done (confirmed):**
- Index redirect changed from `/todos` to `/dashboard` -- correct
- `mod.rs` exports `get_dashboard` and all GTD placeholder routes -- correct
- `startup.rs` wires up `/dashboard`, `/inbox`, `/next-actions`, `/projects`, `/waiting-for`, `/someday-maybe`, `/review` -- correct
- Content max-width bumped from 40rem to 44rem -- correct
- `hx-boost="true"` on `<body>` -- correct
- Placeholder routes with `AuthenticatedUser` guard -- correct, all GTD pages require auth
- Placeholder descriptions are helpful and match GTD language -- good copy

**Product feedback on placeholders:**
The descriptions are good GTD language. "Capture anything on your mind. Process it later." for Inbox is exactly the right tone. These descriptions will eventually become the empty-state messages, so they're pulling double duty nicely.

**Still outstanding from Task #11 description:**
1. **Quick capture input in header** (item #3) -- "Quick capture input in header (POST /inbox)." This is a key part of US-GTD-8: "Quick capture input is available on every authenticated page (in the header, above main content)." Not seeing it yet.
2. **Mobile-first flexbox wrap nav** (item #10) -- CSS for the GTD nav (`.gtd-nav`, `.gtd-nav__list`, `.gtd-nav__link`) needs to be added. Flexbox with wrap so it works on mobile.
3. **GTD nav CSS for `aria-current="page"` styling** -- bottom border accent + semibold for active state (per team decisions).

**Existing auth flows**: The `/todos` route is still registered and working, which is correct -- acceptance criterion #9 says existing auth flows must continue to work. Good.

**One product question**: The test `authenticated_user_index_redirects_to_dashboard` now expects redirect to `/dashboard`. But the existing Playwright e2e tests may expect the old `/todos` redirect. Have you checked whether the existing e2e tests still pass with this change? If they break, we need to update them.
input.summary: Updated product review after latest changes

2. **Mobile-first flexbox wrap nav** (item #10) -- CSS for the GTD nav (`.gtd-nav`, `.gtd-nav__list`, `.gtd-nav__link`) needs to be added. Flexbox with wrap so it works on mobile.
3. **GTD nav CSS for `aria-current="page"` styling** -- bottom border accent + semibold for active state (per team decisions).

**Existing auth flows**: The `/todos` route is still registered and working, which is correct -- acceptance criterion #9 says existing auth flows must continue to work. Good.

**Now done (confirmed):**
- Index redirect changed from `/todos` to `/dashboard` -- correct
- `mod.rs` exports `get_dashboard` and all GTD placeholder routes -- correct
- `startup.rs` wires up `/dashboard`, `/inbox`, `/next-actions`, `/projects`, `/waiting-for`, `/someday-maybe`, `/review` -- correct
- Content max-width bumped from 40rem to 44rem -- correct
- `hx-boost="true"` on `<body>` -- correct
- Placeholder routes with `AuthenticatedUser` guard -- correct, all GTD pages require auth
- Placeholder descriptions are helpful and match GTD language -- good copy

**Product feedback on placeholders:**
The descriptions are good GTD language. "Capture anything on your mind. Process it later." for Inbox is exactly the right tone. These descriptions will eventually become the empty-state messages, so they're pulling double duty nicely.

**Still outstanding from Task #11 description:**
1. **Quick capture input in header** (item #3) -- "Quick capture input in header (POST /inbox)." This is a key part of US-GTD-8: "Quick capture input is available on every authenticated page (in the header, above main content)." Not seeing it yet.
2. **Mobile-first flexbox wrap nav** (item #10) -- CSS for the GTD nav (`.gtd-nav`, `.gtd-nav__list`, `.gtd-nav__link`) needs to be added. Flexbox with wrap so it works on mobile.

content.text:
I've sent updated product feedback to Luca. Here's the current status from my perspective as product reviewer:

**Completed items (verified against US-GTD-8):**
- Dashboard route with summary stats (Inbox, Next Actions, Stalled Projects, Waiting For)
- GTD navigation with all 7 links + `aria-current="page"` + `aria-label="GTD lists"`
- All 6 GTD placeholder routes (authenticated, with good descriptions)
- Index redirect updated to `/dashboard`
- `hx-boost="true"` on body
- Content max-width to 44rem
- Sign out button preserved

**Still outstanding:**
1. Quick capture input in header (US-GTD-8 acceptance criterion + task item #3)
2. CSS for GTD nav (mobile-first flexbox wrap, active state styling)
3. Verification that existing Playwright e2e tests still pass with the `/todos` -> `/dashboard` redirect change

I'm standing by to review further progress and provide consensus when the full implementation is ready.
